evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Manage and prioritize multiple concurrent analytics projects and tasks efficiently
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Mixed: 'efficiently' and improving performance lean velocity while no explicit rigour/testing/quality language is present, keeping the JD light on both signals overall.
evidence.domain_risk: Utilize behaviour analytics and data-driven insights to tangibly improve website performance
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Digital marketing/web analytics with limited, recoverable consequences — no financial reporting or regulatory language.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Proven expertise with tag management platforms: Tealium (preferred), Google Tag Manager, or Adobe Launch
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named enterprise tag-management and analytics tools already deployed in responsibilities suggest an existing, growing digital analytics function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Integrate analytics data with marketing platforms such as Google Ads and major Social Media platforms
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Some named tools and precise tasks (JavaScript tracking, tag management) alongside generic bullets like 'Strong customer focus, teamwork' produce a mixed profile.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Integrate analytics data with marketing platforms such as Google Ads and major Social Media platforms
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role centers on marketing/GTM platform integration and website performance, placing it in commercial.
evidence.autonomy_level: Bridge communication gaps and align objectives effectively between technical and business stakeholders
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Verbs are support/delivery-oriented (manage, bridge, implement, integrate) with no direction-setting or ownership language.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools for the candidate or AI-consuming data infrastructure anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: No testing or data quality signal.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: No testing, data contracts, observability, or data quality language appears anywhere in the JD.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: None: framed entirely in delivery and capability terms with no risk register.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Responsibilities center on improving and integrating existing tracking/analytics setups rather than building new infrastructure from scratch.
